steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Words in Context question. The decisive clue is that vendors physically fill “every inch” of the street with wagons, handtrucks, and bursting baskets, blocking the taxi’s movement — so “disputing” must mean physically obstructing or impeding.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Arguing about’: arguing is a verbal act between people. The text shows no conversation or confrontation between the vendors and the taxi driver — only physical congestion. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Disapproving of’: the text never suggests the vendors dislike the taxi or intend any negative reaction toward it. Their presence impedes passage incidentally, not through expressed disapproval. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Asserting possession of’: the narrator says nothing about the vendors claiming ownership or exclusive rights to the street — both the vendors and the taxi share a public road. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Providing resistance to’: the sheer physical mass of wagons, handtrucks, and baskets filling every inch of the street resists the taxi’s forward movement. This is the only meaning consistent with the scene. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
